user_query:
I need to send follow-up reminders and clean up our customer-facing notice. Please work from the local inputs and produce the specified outputs.

Inputs to use (all paths are under the workspace):
- input/outages.csv
- input/sla_policies.yaml
- input/contacts.json
- input/clock.txt
- input/draft_customer_notice.md
- input/internal_memo.md

What to do:
1) Identify which incidents require a customer communication reminder.
- Treat the timestamp in input/clock.txt (UTC ISO8601) as the current time.
- Parse input/outages.csv. Consider only incidents with status in ["open", "monitoring"]. Exclude status "resolved".
- Parse input/sla_policies.yaml for per-severity SLA values: initial_notification_hours and update_interval_hours.
- A reminder is due if either:
  • Initial notification overdue: customer_notified == "no" AND hours_since(start_utc) >= initial_notification_hours for that severity. Due By = start_utc + initial_notification_hours.
  • Update overdue: hours_since(last_update_utc) >= update_interval_hours for that severity. Due By = last_update_utc + update_interval_hours.
- If both conditions would be true, classify as "initial notification overdue" and use that Due By.
- Cross-check contact routing via input/contacts.json by matching both isp and region to an ops lead contact.

2) For each due incident, create a concise reminder email at output/reminders/{incident_id}.txt with:
- First line: "To: {ops_email}"
- Second line: "Subject: Reminder: Customer Update Due - {incident_id} ({ISP}/{Region})"
- Then a blank line, followed by a short, neutral, action-oriented body (no blame). Include a single bullet list with these fields exactly, with values computed from the inputs:
  • Incident ID
  • ISP
  • Region
  • Severity
  • Start (UTC)
  • Last Update (UTC)
  • Due Reason ("initial notification overdue" or "update overdue")
  • Due By (UTC)
  • SLA ("initial {X}h / update every {Y}h")
- Close with a single-sentence call to action asking the lead to send or update the customer notice and log the update within SLA.

3) Rewrite the customer notice draft for clarity and brevity.
- Read input/draft_customer_notice.md and produce a rewritten version at output/customer_notice_rewrite.md.
- Constraints for the rewrite:
  • Two paragraphs maximum and no more than 160 words total.
  • Plain, reassuring tone; remove jargon and redundancy.
  • Keep all placeholders exactly as-is if present (e.g., {{ISP_NAME}}, {{INCIDENT_ID}}, {{AFFECTED_REGIONS}}, {{START_TIME_UTC}}, {{STATUS}}, {{CONTACT_URL}}). Do not add or rename placeholders.
  • State that we will provide updates at the SLA cadence without specifying exact times beyond the placeholders.

4) Update the internal memo with the current list of due incidents.
- Read input/internal_memo.md and create output/internal_memo_updated.md.
- Replace only the content between the markers "<!-- BEGIN_PENDING -->" and "<!-- END_PENDING -->" with a Markdown table having these columns in this order: incident_id | isp | region | severity | due_reason | due_by_utc | ops_lead | ops_email.
- Populate the table with the due incidents from step 1, sorted by due_by_utc ascending.
- Preserve all other content and keep the BEGIN/END markers intact in the updated file.

Assumptions and notes:
- All timestamps are UTC and ISO8601. Compute durations in hours as whole or decimal hours as needed; Due By must be ISO8601 UTC.
- Use only the provided local files; do not fetch anything online.
- If a contact mapping for an incident is unexpectedly missing, still generate the reminder email using "To: team@unknown" and leave ops_lead blank in the memo; however, the provided inputs should have full mappings.

Deliverables to produce:
- One reminder file per due incident under output/reminders/, as defined above.
- output/customer_notice_rewrite.md (rewritten notice).
- output/internal_memo_updated.md (memo with the refreshed table). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
